| **Kaizen** | Continuous improvement by everyone | 1. Identify waste 2. Propose improvement 3. Implement 4. Measure | Culture |
| **Gemba Walk** | Go to the actual place of work | 1. Observe 2. Ask why 3. Respect 4. Learn | Management |
| **Hoshin Kanri** | Policy deployment, catchball | 1. Strategic plan 2. Department goals 3. Individual targets 4. Review | Alignment |
| **Critical Chain Project Management** | Buffer management, resource constraints | 1. Network diagram 2. Critical chain 3. Buffer 4. Monitor | Project delivery |
| **Agile Portfolio Management** | Strategic themes → Epics → Features → Stories | 1. Investment 2. Initiative 3. Breakdown 4. Execution | Scale |
| **Stage-Gate Process** | Idea → Scoping → Business Case → Development → Testing → Launch → Post-Launch | 1. Concept 2. Preliminary 3. Detailed 4. Build 5. Validate 6. Commercialize 7. Review | NPD |
| **Idea Management** | Capture → Evaluate → Develop → Implement → Measure | 1. Suggestion box 2. Scoring 3. Prototype 4. Pilot 5. Impact | Innovation |
| **Crowdsourcing** | Define → Broadcast → Collect → Evaluate → Reward → Implement | 1. Challenge 2. Platform 3. Ideas 4. Judging 5. Prize 6. Product | Open innovation |
| **Benchmarking** | Plan → Collect → Analyze → Adapt → Implement → Monitor | 1. What to benchmark 2. Data 3. Gap 4. Best practice 5. Change 6. Results | Competitive |
| **Business Process Reengineering (BPR)** | Radical redesign of core processes | 1. As-is 2. To-be 3. Gap 4. Implementation | Transformation |
| **Robotic Process Automation (RPA)** | Identify → Design → Build → Test → Deploy → Monitor → Maintain | 1. Process mining 2. Workflow 3. Bot 4. UAT 5. Production 6. Logging 7. Update | Automation |
| **Intelligent Automation (IA)** | RPA + AI/ML for cognitive tasks | 1. Process 2. AI model 3. Integration 4. Orchestration | Advanced automation |
| **Hyperautomation** | Combine RPA, AI, iBPMS, low-code for end-to-end automation | 1. Discovery 2. Design 3. Automate 4. Measure 5. Optimize | Enterprise automation |
| **Digital Transformation** | Strategy → Customer Experience → Operations → Culture → Technology | 1. Vision 2. Journey map 3. Process digitization 4. Change management 5. Platform | Transformation |
| **Change Management (Kotter)** | Urgency → Coalition → Vision → Communicate → Empower → Quick Wins → Sustain → Anchor | 1. Case for change 2. Guiding team 3. Direction 4. Buy-in 5. Barrier removal 6. Momentum 7. Persistence 8. Culture | Organizational change |
| **ADKAR** | Awareness → Desire → Knowledge → Ability → Reinforcement | 1. Why 2. Want 3. How 4. Can 5. Sustain | Individual change |
| **Prosci 3-Phase Process** | Prepare Approach → Manage Change → Sustain Outcomes | 1. Strategy 2. Implementation 3. Reinforcement | Structured change |
| **Lewin's Change Model** | Unfreeze → Change → Refreeze | 1. Motivation 2. Transition 3. Stability | Classic change |
| **McKinsey 7-S** | Strategy, Structure, Systems, Shared Values, Skills, Style, Staff | 1. Alignment assessment 2. Gap 3. Intervention | Organizational alignment |
| **Galbraith Star Model** | Strategy → Structure → Processes → Rewards → People | 1. Design 2. Fit 3. Implementation | Organization design |
| **Nadler-Tushman Congruence Model** | Input → Transformation → Output with feedback | 1. Environment 2. Work 3. People 4. Formal/informal 5. Fit | Diagnostic |
| **Burke-Litwin Model** | Causal model of organizational performance | 1. External environment 2. Leadership 3. Strategy 4. Culture 5. Systems 6. Performance | Causal analysis |
| **Weisbord Six-Box Model** | Purposes, Structure, Rewards, Helpful Mechanisms, Relationships, Leadership, Environment | 1. Diagnosis 2. Intervention | Diagnostic |
| **PESTLE Analysis Workflow** | Political → Economic → Social → Technological → Legal → Environmental | 1. Factor identification 2. Impact assessment 3. Trend analysis 4. Strategic response | Macro analysis |
| **Scenario Planning** | Define Scope → Identify Drivers → Develop Scenarios → Assess Implications → Monitor | 1. Time horizon 2. STEEP 3. Narrative 4. Strategy 5. Indicators | Future planning |
| **War Gaming** | Define → Research → Play → Debrief → Apply | 1. Scenario 2. Intelligence 3. Simulation 4. Lessons 5. Action | Competitive |
| **Red Teaming** | Objective → Plan → Execute → Report → Remediate | 1. Scope 2. Attack simulation 3. Findings 4. Report 5. Fix | Security assessment |
| **Tabletop Exercise** | Scenario → Discussion → Decision → Evaluation → Improvement | 1. Incident 2. Response 3. Choice 4. Assessment 5. Plan update | Preparedness |
| **Business Continuity Planning** | BIA → Risk Assessment → Strategy → Plan → Testing → Maintenance | 1. Impact 2. Threat 3. Recovery 4. Documentation 5. Drill 6. Update | Resilience |
| **Disaster Recovery Planning** | RPO/RTO → Strategy → Solution → Test → Document → Maintain | 1. Recovery objectives 2. Hot/warm/cold 3. Technology 4. Validation 5. Runbook 6. Refresh | IT resilience |
| **Incident Response** | Preparation → Detection → Analysis → Containment → Eradication → Recovery → Lessons | 1. Playbook 2. Alert 3. Investigation 4. Isolate 5. Remove 6. Restore 7. Post-mortem | Cybersecurity |
| **Vulnerability Management** | Discover → Assess → Prioritize → Remediate → Verify → Report | 1. Scan 2. CVSS 3. Risk 4. Patch 5. Test 6. Metrics | Security ops |
| **Patch Management** | Identify → Evaluate → Test → Approve → Deploy → Verify | 1. Advisory 2. Impact 3. UAT 4. Change 5. Rollout 6. Confirmation | Maintenance |
| **Asset Management** | Identify → Classify → Track → Maintain → Dispose → Audit | 1. Inventory 2. Criticality 3. CMDB 4. Service 5. Retirement 6. Compliance | ITAM |
| **Configuration Management** | Plan → Identify → Control → Status Accounting → Verification → Audit | 1. Baseline 2. CI 3. Change 4. Reporting 5. Audit 6. Compliance | CM |
| **Release Management** | Plan → Build → Test → Deploy → Verify → Close | 1. Scope 2. CI/CD 3. QA 4. Production 5. Monitoring 6. Retrospective | Software delivery |
| **Service Request Management** | Intake → Approval → Fulfillment → Closure → Satisfaction | 1. Catalog 2. Authorization 3. Provisioning 4. Confirmation 5. Survey | ITSM |
| **Problem Management** | Detection → Logging → Investigation → Diagnosis → Workaround → Resolution → Closure | 1. Incident pattern 2. Ticket 3. Root cause 4. Known error 5. Temporary fix 6. Permanent 7. Review | ITSM |
| **Change Management (ITIL)** | RFC → Assessment → Approval → Implementation → Review → Closure | 1. Request 2. CAB 3. Authorization 4. Deployment 5. PIR 6. Close | ITSM |
| **Capacity Management** | Monitor → Analyze → Forecast → Plan → Implement → Review | 1. Utilization 2. Trend 3. Growth 4. Procurement 5. Scaling 6. Optimization | ITSM |
| **Availability Management** | Requirements → Design → Implementation → Testing → Monitoring → Improvement | 1. SLA 2. Architecture 3. Build 4. HA test 5. Uptime 6. Review | ITSM |
| **Service Level Management** | Define → Negotiate → Monitor → Report → Review → Improve | 1. SLA 2. Agreement 3. Tracking 4. Dashboard 5. Meeting 6. Adjustment | ITSM |
| **Financial Management (IT)** | Budget → Chargeback → Showback → Investment → Cost Optimization | 1. Plan 2. Allocation 3. Transparency 4. ROI 5. Efficiency | IT financial |
| **Vendor Management** | Selection → Contract → Onboarding → Performance → Review → Renewal/Exit | 1. RFP 2. Negotiation 3. Integration 4. KPI 5. Assessment 6. Decision | Procurement |
| **Contract Lifecycle Management** | Draft → Negotiate → Approve → Execute → Monitor → Renew/Terminate | 1. Template 2. Redline 3. Legal 4. Signature 5. Compliance 6. Action | Legal ops |
| **Knowledge Management** | Create → Capture → Organize → Share → Apply → Evaluate | 1. Content 2. Repository 3. Taxonomy 4. Portal 5. Workflow 6. Impact | Organizational learning |
| **Document Management** | Create → Review → Approve → Publish → Distribute → Retain/Dispose | 1. Draft 2. Collaboration 3. Sign-off 4. Version control 5. Access 6. Retention | Records |
| **Records Management** | Classification → Storage → Retrieval → Retention → Disposition → Audit | 1. Taxonomy 2. Archive 3. Search 4. Schedule 5. Destruction 6. Compliance | Compliance |
| **Email Management** | Receive → Triage → Action → File → Follow-up → Archive/Delete | 1. Inbox 2. Priority 3. Response 4. Folder 5. Reminder 6. Retention | Productivity |
| **Meeting Management** | Objective → Agenda → Invite → Facilitate → Notes → Action Items → Follow-up | 1. Purpose 2. Topics 3. Attendees 4. Timekeeping 5. Minutes 6. Tasks 7. Review | Collaboration |

### 4.3 Project, Product & Software Delivery
| Name | Workflow | Steps | Hypertaks Application |
|------|----------|-------|-----------------------|
| **Project Management (PMBOK)** | Initiate → Plan → Execute → Monitor & Control → Close | 1. Charter 2. WBS 3. Work 4. Tracking 5. Handover | Traditional PM |
| **Agile Project Management** | Vision → Product Backlog → Sprint Planning → Daily Standup → Sprint Review → Retrospective | 1. Goal 2. Stories 3. Commitment 4. Sync 5. Demo 6. Improve | Agile PM |
| **Scrum** | Product Owner → Scrum Master → Dev Team → Sprints → Artifacts → Events | Roles, 2-week sprint, backlog, increment | Agile framework |
| **Kanban** | Visualize → Limit WIP → Manage Flow → Make Policies Explicit → Improve Collaboratively | Board, columns, cards, WIP limits | Flow management |
| **Scrumban** | Scrum structure + Kanban flow | Sprint + continuous flow hybrid | Flexibility |
| **SAFe (Scaled Agile Framework)** | Portfolio → Large Solution → Program → Team | Alignment across enterprise | Scale agile |
| **LeSS (Large-Scale Scrum)** | One product backlog, one definition of done, one product owner | Simplified scaling | Scale agile |
| **Nexus** | 3-9 Scrum teams integrated | Integration team | Scale agile |
| **Disciplined Agile (DA)** | Goal-driven, context-sensitive | Choose your way | Pragmatic agile |
| **Spotify Model** | Squads, Tribes, Chapters, Guilds | Autonomy with alignment | Organizational agile |
| **DevOps Lifecycle** | Plan → Create → Verify → Preprod → Release → Configure → Monitor | Continuous flow | Software delivery |
| **GitOps Workflow** | Git as source of truth → PR → CI → Merge → CD → Monitor | Declarative, versioned, automated | Infrastructure |
| **DataOps** | Data ingestion → Processing → Quality → Deployment → Monitoring → Improvement | Data pipeline automation | Data engineering |
| **MLOps Lifecycle** | Data → Model → Code → Pipeline → Deploy → Monitor → Retrain | ML production lifecycle | ML engineering |
| **AIOps** | Data ingestion → Pattern detection → Anomaly → Root cause → Automation → Prediction | AI for IT operations | Intelligent ops |
| **FinOps** | Inform → Optimize → Operate → Cloud financial management | Visibility, rate reduction, usage | Cloud cost |
| **GreenOps** | Measure → Reduce → Offset → Report → Improve | Carbon footprint of cloud | Sustainability |
| **SecOps** | Prevent → Detect → Respond → Recover → Learn | Security operations | Defense |
| **NetOps** | Monitor → Analyze → Automate → Optimize → Secure | Network operations | Connectivity |
| **DevSecOps Pipeline** | Plan (security req) → Code (SAST) → Build (SCA) → Test (DAST) → Deploy (config scan) → Operate (monitor) → Monitor (threat intel) | Shift-left security | Secure delivery |
| **SRE Practices** | Error Budget → SLO → SLI → Alerting → Toil Reduction → Blameless Postmortem | Reliability engineering | Google-style ops |
| **Chaos Engineering Workflow** | Steady State → Hypothesis → Experiment → Blast Radius → Abort Condition → Learn → Fix | Controlled failure | Resilience |
| **Platform Engineering** | Internal Developer Platform → Self-Service → Golden Paths → Standardization → Product mindset | Developer experience | Platform team |
| **Developer Experience (DX)** | Onboarding → Documentation → Tooling → Feedback Loop → Support → Community | Developer satisfaction | Productivity |
| **API Lifecycle Management** | Design → Mock → Develop → Test → Document → Deploy → Version → Deprecate | API product management | API platform |
| **API Governance** | Standards → Review → Catalog → Monitor → Enforce → Evolve | Consistency | API quality |
| **API Monetization** | Free → Freemium → Tiered → Usage-Based → Enterprise → Partner | Revenue from APIs | API business |
| **API Marketplace** | Provider onboarding → Discovery → Try → Subscribe → Manage → Rate/Review | Ecosystem | API distribution |
| **Integration Platform as a Service (iPaaS)** | Connect → Transform → Orchestrate → Monitor → Manage | Cloud integration | Enterprise connectivity |
| **Enterprise Service Bus (ESB)** | Route → Transform → Mediate → Orchestrate → Monitor | Legacy integration | Middleware |
| **Microservices Governance** | Design standards → Service catalog → Inter-service contract → Observability → Security → Deprecation | Distributed governance | Microservices |

| **Personal Branding** | Position → Content → Network → Engagement → Visibility → Opportunity → Measure | Individual | Career |
| **Reputation Management** | Monitor → Assess → Strategy → Action → Measure → Report → Protect → Enhance | Perception | Trust |
| **Digital Reputation** | Search → Social → Review → Response → SEO → Content → Monitor → Improve | Online | Brand |
| **Review Management** | Monitor → Respond → Escalate → Encourage → Analyze → Improve → Report | Customer reviews | Local SEO |
| **Influencer Relations** | Identify → Vet → Brief → Content → Approval → Publish → Amplify → Measure → Pay | Creator | Authenticity |
| **Community Management** | Platform → Guidelines → Onboarding → Engagement → Moderation → Events → Growth → Monetize | Online community | Retention |
| **Forum / Discussion Board** | Setup → Categories → Moderation → Quality → Engagement → Recognition → Archive | Community | Support |
| **User Group** | Identify → Invite → Event → Content → Network → Feedback → Advocate → Measure | Power users | Advocacy |
| **Ambassador Program** | Recruit → Train → Activate → Content → Reward → Measure → Optimize → Scale | Super fans | Word of mouth |
| **Referral Program** | Design → Incentive → Tracking → Promotion → Conversion → Reward → Fraud → Optimize | Viral growth | Acquisition |
| **Loyalty Program** | Design → Earn → Burn → Tier → Personalize → Communicate → Measure → Evolve | Retention | Repeat |
| **Gamification** | Objectives → Behaviors → Mechanics → Rewards → Feedback → Social → Progress → Optimize | Engagement | Motivation |

### 4.10 Behavior & Decision-Making
| Name | Workflow | Steps | Hypertaks Application |
|------|----------|-------|-----------------------|
| **Behavioral Economics in Product** | Nudge → Default → Social Proof → Scarcity → Loss Aversion → Framing → Feedback → Iterate | Psychology | Conversion |
| **Habit Formation** | Trigger → Action → Variable Reward → Investment → Habit → Loop → Craving → Behavior | Nir Eyal | Retention |
| **Hook Model** | Trigger → Action → Reward → Investment | Habit design | Engagement |
| **Fogg Behavior Model** | Motivation + Ability + Prompt = Behavior | BJ Fogg | Behavior change |
| **BJT (Behavioral Journey Tracking)** | Awareness → Consideration → Trial → Adoption → Habit → Advocacy → Exit | User journey | Lifecycle |
| **Psychological Safety** | Inclusion → Learner → Contributor → Challenger → Team → Performance → Innovation | Amy Edmondson | Culture |
| **Growth Mindset** | Challenge → Effort → Feedback → Learning → Resilience → Achievement → Fulfillment | Carol Dweck | Development |
| **Flow State** | Challenge-skill balance → Clear goals → Immediate feedback → Deep concentration → Control → Transformation | Mihaly Csikszentmihalyi | Productivity |
| **Deep Work** | Ritual → Focus → Eliminate distraction → Batch shallow → Rest → Measure → Optimize | Cal Newport | Knowledge work |
| **Pomodoro Technique** | Task → 25 min focus → 5 min break → Repeat → Long break → Review → Adjust | Time management | Focus |
| **Time Blocking** | Priorities → Calendar blocks → Protect → Execute → Review → Adjust → Repeat | Scheduling | Productivity |
| **Eat the Frog** | Hardest task first → Momentum → Easier tasks → Completion → Satisfaction → Habit | Priority | Procrastination |
| **Getting Things Done (GTD)** | Capture → Clarify → Organize → Reflect → Engage | David Allen | Personal productivity |
| **PARA Method** | Projects → Areas → Resources → Archives | Tiago Forte | Information organization |
| **Zettelkasten** | Fleeting → Literature → Permanent → Index → Connect → Develop → Write | Niklas Luhmann | Knowledge management |
| **Building a Second Brain** | Capture → Organize → Distill → Express | Tiago Forte | Personal KM |
| **Progressive Summarization** | Layer 1: Capture → Layer 2: Bold → Layer 3: Highlight → Layer 4: Summary → Layer 5: Remix | Information hierarchy | Reading |
| **Smart Notes** | Literature → Permanent → Project → Index → Develop → Publish | Sönke Ahrens | Academic writing |
| **Barbell Strategy** | Safe + Speculative, nothing in between | Nassim Taleb | Risk management |
| **Antifragility** | Gain from disorder → Stressors → Optionality → Convexity → Evolution | Nassim Taleb | Resilience |
| **Skin in the Game** | Alignment through personal risk → Accountability → Symmetry → Ethics | Nassim Taleb | Governance |
| **Via Negativa** | Improvement by subtraction → Remove → Simplify → Essentialize | Minimalism | Focus |
| **Inversion** | Think backwards → Avoid stupidity → Prevent failure → Solve by negation | Charlie Munger | Decision |
| **Second-Order Thinking** | Consequences of consequences → Systemic → Long-term → Unintended | Howard Marks | Strategy |
| **First Principles Thinking** | Break down to fundamentals → Rebuild → Question assumptions → Original | Aristotle/Elon Musk | Innovation |
| **Mental Models** | Map → Territory → Inversion → Occam → Hanlon → Parkinson → Peter → Dunning-Kruger → etc. | Charlie Munger | Wisdom |
| **Map vs Territory** | Model is not reality → Update → Falsify → Adapt | Korzybski | Epistemology |
| **Hanlon's Razor** | Never attribute to malice what can be explained by stupidity | Robert Hanlon | Assumption |
| **Parkinson's Law** | Work expands to fill time available | Cyril Northcote Parkinson | Time management |
| **Peter Principle** | People rise to level of incompetence | Laurence Peter | Organization |
| **Dunning-Kruger Effect** | Incompetence → Overconfidence → Competence → Underconfidence → Mastery | Kruger & Dunning | Self-awareness |
| **Confirmation Bias** | Seek confirming evidence → Challenge → Disconfirm → Falsify | Psychology | Objectivity |
| **Availability Heuristic** | Judge frequency by ease of recall → Base rate → Statistics → Evidence | Tversky & Kahneman | Rationality |
| **Anchoring Bias** | Over-rely on first piece of information → Adjust → Independent estimate | Tversky & Kahneman | Negotiation |
| **Sunk Cost Fallacy** | Continue due to past investment → Marginal analysis → Future value → Exit | Economics | Decision |
| **Loss Aversion** | Losses loom larger than gains → Reframe → Risk-neutral → Expected value | Tversky & Kahneman | Psychology |
| **Endowment Effect** | Overvalue what you own → Market price → Willingness to pay → Willingness to accept | Thaler | Negotiation |
| **Status Quo Bias** | Prefer current state → Default → Opt-in → Active choice | Samuelson & Zeckhauser | Change |
| **Framing Effect** | Presentation affects choice → Reframe → Absolute → Relative | Tversky & Kahneman | Communication |
| **Reciprocity Principle** | Obligation to return favors → Give → Receive → Social capital | Cialdini | Influence |
| **Social Proof** | Follow the crowd → Independent judgment → Base rate → Evidence | Cialdini | Persuasion |
| **Scarcity Principle** | Value increases with scarcity → Genuine scarcity → Urgency → Action | Cialdini | Marketing |
| **Authority Principle** | Defer to experts → Credential → Expertise → Track record | Cialdini | Trust |
| **Consistency Principle** | Desire to be consistent with past actions → Small commitment → Escalation | Cialdini | Persuasion |
| **Liking Principle** | Agree with people we like → Rapport → Similarity → Compliment | Cialdini | Relationship |
| **Unity Principle** | Shared identity → Us vs them → In-group → Belonging | Cialdini | Community |
| **Nudge Theory** | Choice architecture → Default → Simplify → Social proof → Feedback → Commitment | Thaler & Sunstein | Behavioral design |
| **Libertarian Paternalism** | Preserve choice → Guide toward better outcome → Transparency → Opt-out | Thaler & Sunstein | Policy |
| **System 1 vs System 2** | Fast intuitive vs slow deliberate → Recognize → Override → Check | Kahneman | Thinking |
| **Pre-Mortem** | Imagine failure → Work backwards → Identify threats → Prevent → Plan | Gary Klein | Risk |
| **Red Team Analysis** | Challenge own assumptions → Adversarial → Devil's advocate → Stress test | Military intelligence | Strategy |
| **Devil's Advocate** | Argue opposite position → Strengthen → Weakness → Robustness | Catholic Church | Decision |
| **Dialectical Inquiry** | Thesis → Antithesis → Synthesis | Hegel | Truth |
| **Delphi Method** | Expert panel → Iterative → Convergence → Forecast | RAND | Prediction |
| **Backcasting** | Define future → Work backwards → Milestones → Actions → Today | Sustainability | Planning |
| **Forecasting** | Data → Model → Prediction → Interval → Update → Accuracy | Statistics | Prediction |
| **Prediction Market** | Wisdom of crowds → Incentives → Aggregation → Accuracy | Hanson | Collective intelligence |
| **Superforecasting** | Fermi estimate → Outside view → Inside view → Update → Brier score | Tetlock | Accuracy |
| **Fermi Estimation** | Break down → Estimate → Multiply → Order of magnitude | Enrico Fermi | Approximation |
| **Reference Class Forecasting** | Similar projects → Base rate → Adjust → Probability | Flyvbjerg | Planning |
| **Planning Fallacy** | Underestimate time/cost → Reference class → Pessimistic → Buffer | Kahneman & Tversky | Project |
| **Optimism Bias** | Overestimate positive outcomes → Realistic → Sensitivity → Scenario | Tali Sharot | Risk |

| **Usage-Based Pricing** | Pay per use → Align value → Low barrier → Scale → Transparency → Fairness | - | Cloud |
| **Outcome-Based Pricing** | Pay for result → Risk transfer → Alignment → Trust → Measurement → Guarantee | - | Enterprise |
| **Dynamic Pricing** | Real-time adjustment → Demand → Supply → Competition → Algorithm → Revenue optimization | - | Airlines, Uber |
| **Surge Pricing** | High demand → Price increase → Supply response → Equilibrium → Controversy | - | Ride-sharing |
| **Penetration Pricing** | Low initial → Market share → Scale → Cost reduction → Gradual increase | - | Entry |
| **Price Skimming** | High initial → Early adopters → Gradual decrease → Segmentation → Maximization | - | Innovation |
| **Value-Based Pricing** | Customer perceived value → Willingness to pay → Price → Profit → Communication | - | Premium |
| **Cost-Plus Pricing** | Cost → Markup → Price → Simple → Fair → Risk-averse | - | Commodity |
| **Competitive Pricing** | Competitor price → Match → Undercut → Premium → Positioning | - | Market |
| **Psychological Pricing** | Charm pricing → Anchoring → Decoy → Bundle → Prestige → Reference | - | Consumer |
| **Price Discrimination** | First-degree (personal) → Second-degree (quantity) → Third-degree (group) → Perfect | - | Revenue |
| **Two-Part Tariff** | Fixed fee + Usage fee → Membership + Consumption → Club goods | - | Gyms, Amazon Prime |
| **Tying & Bundling** | Require purchase of B with A → Leverage market power → Antitrust concern | - | Platform |
| **Versioning** | Different versions → Self-selection → Price discrimination → No cannibalization | - | Software |
| **Auction Pricing** | English → Dutch → First-price sealed → Vickrey → Combinatorial → Double | - | Market clearing |
| **Yield Management** | Perishable inventory → Forecast → Overbooking → Price → Segment → Optimize | - | Airlines, hotels |
| **Revenue Management** | Demand forecasting → Price → Inventory → Capacity → Channel → Optimization | - | Hospitality |
| **Total Revenue Management** | All revenue streams → Holistic → Customer → Channel → Time → Optimization | - | Integrated |

### 4.15 Finance, Risk & Economics
| Name | Workflow | Steps | Hypertaks Application |
|------|----------|-------|-----------------------|
| **Profitability Analysis** | Revenue → Variable cost → Contribution → Fixed cost → Operating profit → Net profit | - | Financial |
| **Break-Even Analysis** | Fixed cost / (Price - Variable cost) → Volume → Target → Sensitivity | - | Planning |
| **Margin Analysis** | Gross → Operating → Net → Contribution → Segment → Product → Customer | - | Performance |
| **Cost-Benefit Analysis** | Identify → Quantify → Monetize → Compare → Decision → Sensitivity | - | Investment |
| **Return on Investment (ROI)** | (Gain - Cost) / Cost → Percentage → Compare → Rank → Decision | - | Capital |
| **Net Present Value (NPV)** | Future cash flows → Discount → Sum → Decision rule → Sensitivity | - | Valuation |
| **Internal Rate of Return (IRR)** | Discount rate → NPV = 0 → Compare → Hurdle rate → Decision | - | Return |
| **Payback Period** | Initial investment / Annual cash flow → Time → Risk → Liquidity → Decision | - | Risk |
| **Profitability Index** | NPV / Initial investment → Ratio → Rank → Capital rationing | - | Efficiency |
| **Real Options Analysis** | Option to defer → Expand → Abandon → Switch → Value flexibility → Strategic NPV | - | Uncertainty |
| **Decision Tree Analysis** | Decision nodes → Chance nodes → Probabilities → Payoffs → Rollback → Optimal | - | Sequential |
| **Scenario Analysis** | Best → Base → Worst case → Assumptions → Probability → Strategy → Contingency | - | Planning |
| **Stress Testing** | Extreme scenario → Impact → Vulnerability → Capital → Recovery → Plan | - | Resilience |
| **Value at Risk (VaR)** | Confidence level → Time horizon → Maximum loss → Historical → Variance-covariance → Monte Carlo | - | Risk |
| **Expected Shortfall (CVaR)** | Average loss beyond VaR → Tail risk → Coherent measure → Regulation | - | Risk |
| **Credit Risk Modeling** | Probability of default → Loss given default → Exposure at default → Expected loss | - | Lending |
| **Operational Risk** | Internal fraud → External fraud → Employment → Clients → Damage → Disruption → Execution | Basel II | Risk |
| **Market Risk** | Interest rate → Equity → Foreign exchange → Commodity → Volatility → Correlation | - | Trading |
| **Liquidity Risk** | Funding → Market → Contagion → Run → Central bank → Lender of last resort | - | Banking |
| **Systemic Risk** | Institution → Contagion → Cascade → Market failure → Macroprudential → Regulation | - | Financial stability |
| **Moral Hazard in Finance** | Risk-taking → Protection → Asymmetric information → Regulation → Incentive alignment | - | Post-crisis |
| **Adverse Selection in Insurance** | High-risk → Insurance → Pool deterioration → Mandate → Screening → Signaling | - | Insurance |
| **Principal-Agent in Finance** | Manager → Shareholder → Asymmetric info → Incentive → Monitoring → Governance | - | Corporate |
| **Information Asymmetry** | Hidden action → Hidden type → Market failure → Signaling → Screening → Mechanism design | Akerlof, Spence, Stiglitz | Markets |
| **Market Failure** | Externalities → Public goods → Monopoly → Information asymmetry → Government intervention | - | Policy |
| **Public Choice Theory** | Voters → Politicians → Bureaucrats → Self-interest → Rent-seeking → Regulation | Buchanan & Tullock | Governance |
| **Regulatory Capture** | Regulated → Regulator → Revolving door → Industry influence → Weak regulation | Stigler | Reform |
| **Rent-Seeking** | Lobby → Regulation → Barrier → Monopoly profit → Deadweight loss → Corruption | Krueger | Efficiency |
| **Creative Destruction** | Innovation → Obsolescence → Reallocation → Growth → Dynamism → Disruption | Schumpeter | Economy |
| **Knowledge Spillover** | R&D → External benefit → Innovation cluster → Agglomeration → Growth → Policy | Romer | Innovation policy |
| **Endogenous Growth Theory** | R&D → Human capital → Innovation → Policy → Growth → Non-diminishing returns | Romer, Lucas | Development |
| **Solow Growth Model** | Capital → Labor → Technology → Steady state → Convergence → Savings | Solow | Macroeconomics |
| **New Growth Theory** | Ideas → Non-rival → Increasing returns → Scale → Policy → Institutions | Romer | Policy |
| **Institutional Economics** | Rules → Transaction costs → Property rights → Enforcement → Development → History | North, Williamson | Development |
| **Development Economics** | Poverty → Growth → Inequality → Health → Education → Institutions → Policy | - | Global dev |
| **Behavioral Development** | Psychology → Decision-making → Poverty traps → Nudges → Financial inclusion → Education | - | Development |
| **Randomized Controlled Trials (RCT) in Development** | Treatment → Control → Randomization → Impact → Cost-effectiveness → Scale → Policy | J-PAL, IPA | Evidence |
| **Social Impact Bond (SIB)** | Investor → Service provider → Outcome → Government payment → Risk transfer → Social good | - | Social finance |
| **Development Impact Bond (DIB)** | Similar to SIB but outcome payer is donor/philanthropist | - | Development |
| **Pay for Success** | Outcome-based funding → Prevention → Measurement → Payment → Innovation | - | Social services |
| **Results-Based Financing** | Performance → Verification → Payment → Incentive → Accountability → Efficiency | - | Development |
| **Conditional Cash Transfer** | Cash → Conditions (education, health) → Human capital → Poverty reduction → Evaluation | - | Social protection |
| **Universal Basic Income (UBI)** | Unconditional cash → Poverty → Inequality → Automation → Experiment → Policy | - | Future of work |
| **Job Guarantee** | Government → Employment → Skills → Demand → Multiplier → Dignity | - | Employment |
| **Progressive Taxation** | Ability to pay → Redistribution → Inequality → Revenue → Public goods → Incentive | - | Fiscal |
| **Wealth Tax** | Net wealth → Annual levy → Inequality → Capital flight → Valuation → Exemption | - | Redistribution |
| **Carbon Tax** | Emissions → Price → Pigouvian → Reduction → Revenue → Dividend | - | Climate |
| **Cap-and-Trade** | Cap → Allowances → Trade → Market → Price → Efficiency → Innovation | - | Climate |
| **Border Carbon Adjustment** | Import tariff → Carbon content → Competitiveness → Leakage → WTO → Implementation | - | Trade & climate |
| **Green Industrial Policy** | Subsidy → Technology → Scale → Cost reduction → Export → Competitiveness | - | Industrial policy |
| **Just Transition** | Fossil workers → Retraining → New jobs → Community → Support → Equity → Participation | - | Climate justice |
| **Climate Finance** | Mitigation → Adaptation → Loss & damage → Green climate fund → Private → Public | - | Climate |
| **Loss and Damage** | Climate impacts → Compensation → Liability → Negotiation → Fund → Mechanism | - | Climate justice |
| **Nature-Based Solutions** | Ecosystem → Conservation → Restoration → Management → Carbon → Biodiversity → Livelihood | - | Conservation |
| **Circular Economy Business Models** | Access → Product-as-a-service → Sharing → Remanufacturing → Recycling → Cascade | - | Sustainability |
| **Industrial Symbiosis** | Waste → Input → By-product exchange → Cluster → Network → Policy → Scale | - | Circular |
| **Biomimicry** | Nature → Inspiration → Design → Innovation → Sustainability → Efficiency → Resilience | - | Design |
| **Cradle to Cradle** | Waste = food → Biological cycle → Technical cycle → Design → Certification → Innovation | McDonough & Braungart | Sustainability |
| **Triple Bottom Line** | People → Planet → Profit → Reporting → Balance → Stakeholder → Accountability | Elkington | CSR |
| **Integrated Reporting** | Financial → Manufactured → Intellectual → Human → Social → Relationship → Natural → Strategy | IIRC | Reporting |
| **Sustainability Accounting** | ESG metrics → Materiality → Data → Assurance → Standard → Benchmark → Decision | - | Accounting |

| **Palma Ratio** | Top 10% / Bottom 40% → Income concentration → Policy → Middle class → Development | - | Inequality |
| **Kuznets Curve** | Inequality ↑ then ↓ as income rises → Transition → Policy → Industrialization → Inequality | Kuznets | Development |
| **Piketty's r > g** | Return on capital > Growth → Wealth concentration → Inheritance → Tax → Policy | Piketty | Inequality |
